item.title: Re: +150 en Espanol!
item.content:encoded: Huh, convenient timing I guess :P

item.title: +150 en Espanol!
item.content:encoded:
Celebrating passing the +150 point a little late, but better that than never!

In any case, celebrating the Spanish translation by berstager right on time! WOOOOOOO!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Want to get the real scoop behind what's happening at Site-3408? Well, like the night man said, just relax. We are Programmed to Receive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is interesting, but I feel there's no reason the Foundation can't work around the AI editing the database already. Maybe it would work better if there was an official article detailing the anomaly and then the brochure article as supplementary info, but the fact the Foundation hasn't fixed this seems out of place in a world where they're already doing so much. Perhaps if the foundation setup a fake database entry that Site-3408 had editing privileges to separate from the legit entry it would work better.

item.title: yyyeeeeAAAAAA!
item.content:encoded: I fucking love the hotel California references

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure what the anomaly is here; the site itself, the thing writing the brochure, or whatever built the site.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Mission Success :|

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Researcher reaction is Me_irl

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fits in not only with my SCP canon but my hotel california canon. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1665

what the fuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuuudge

I seriously didn't imagine this article would be successful, much less hit +100. Thank you to all you wild, crazy people what made this possible! :D I have decided to celebrate this accomplishmemt in two ways.

First, I +1'd my own article, because I am a prideful, self-aggrandizing tool.

And second, I have done a thing. A horrible, terrible, rotten, vile thing.

Cheers! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crossword SCiP? As in the infinite Crossword SCiP, or something else? Also, make like a Marvin and gimme a link, please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And in the testing chambers

D-Class lives have ceased

They stab 682 with their steely knives

But they just can't kill the beast!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As Roget once said to me about my crossword skip: "Alright…this is juuuust gimmicky enough to amuse me."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to Containment Site California!

Such a lovely place, (Such a lovely place),

Such a lovely face.

Plenty of cells at Containment Site California!

Any time of year, (Any time of year),

You cant flee from here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay!

item.title: Re: Re-Formatator
item.content:encoded: Uuuuuugh, why must everything be so difficult? I should really just stop futzing around with it until I get full internet back. Sitting just shy of +50, so obviously I'm the only one who really cares about the formatting anyway. Removed the quoteblocks, and now it's time to leave well enough alone so I don't drive myself completely crazy.

item.title: Re: Re-Formatator
item.content:encoded:
The image blocks aren't within the quoteblock divs is the problem.

It's never going to line up perfectly unless you use some custom formatting.

item.title: Re-Formatator
item.content:encoded: Okay, I think I figured out a way to get all the images to be lined up with their relevant paragraphs. It doesn't look too bad on mobile to me, but please let me know if it looks like crap to any of y'all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having just gone back through Dmatix's skips to find that I remember and have upvoted a few of 'em, I'm gonna go with it being a good thing, thankya!

I've actually read Vacation Opportunity, but sadly I both forgot about it and apparently didn't upvote it at the time. Both of these issues have been rectified now, and should I go through with plans I'm formulating to write a tale about Site-3408, VO will also be getting a mention and link in said story!

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
You could fix some of the formatting awkwardness at the very bottom of the page by using…

 @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@ @@

…to force spaces and push the navigation links down below the image (and thus centering them). If you copy paste what I put above you oughta create enough space. Not a huge deal but it does look a bit off right now.

Edit: Not applicable given the reformatting of the page. Either way, good work on the skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time I read the description, I hear the Ren and Stimpy "Happy-Go-Lively" music playing in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And they say the Foundation is not a hotel. Feels hotel enough here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it bad that I thought this was a Dmatix skip at first? This seemed to hit all of the meta aspects that Dmatix usually hits. Or maybe it's a compliment, because Dmatix is an amazing author and so are you.

This is pretty much what I had envisioned when I wrote Vacation Opportunity. The thin veil of the resort covering the obvious eldritch horror here greatly appeals to me. Although, if I were to do anything different, I would've added hints of the wrongness from the start. But this is still great.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did heavily consider making this a -J, actually, but I feel that this version wouldn't have made a very good -J. For that, I would've had to ham it up quite a bit more, and I decided I wanted to ride the line between silly and creepy instead. I probably1 didn't ride that line finely enough for some folks, but ah well.

Just to say that this is one of those very rare times when I absolutely agree that "This should have been a -J" is a perfectly legitimate critique. I coulda done, and maybe shoulda done, but I didn't, so I (and Site-3408) have to live with that.

Footnotes
1. obviously

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The anomaly is obvious, counter to what others are saying. But I'm going to march out the tired complaint of "this should be a -J". It's just a little too silly, overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too immediately ridiculous for me. I can usually get behind this kind of thing if there's a slower buildup, but here there's no time for me to dip my toe in the water before a clown jumps out at me, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that sounds more appropriate while still feeling eerie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I don't believe a level of subtely in either direction is necessarily a good or bad thing, I do absolutely understand a personal preference toward one or the other, so I shan't argue the point with you or Decibelle.

As for the Church of the Second Hytoth … I won't argue that either, as I actually agree with you. My original intention wasn't really for it to mean anything lethal, as "blood sacrifice" could simply mean a sacrifice of blood and nothing else. Looking back on it, however, the wording would still prompt most folks to think killing, so I changed it to "blood-letting" instead. Still pretty creepy, I think, but yeah, more in line with what has been shown of the church so far.

Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One thing to note is that the Church of the Second Hytoth is pacifistic in nature and won't kill people to give blood to Rakmou-leusan (unless it's extreme desperation, like with SCP-2417), but I do imagine other past Ortothan groups (and known alien sects) have been more violent with that stuff so this isn't a big issue for me.

Other than that I will agree with Decibelle on this being unsubtle, but I still enjoyed it so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just not feeling it. Feels all too silly and on-the-nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spoilers are now up on The Leak, if you're into it! ->

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah, I would be too! :D Alas, I am only human, so I wrote it up in OpenOffice on my computer, uploaded the file to my phone, copy/pasted the text onto the site, crossed my fingers and hoped for the best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spoilers are now up in The Leak if ya want em! ->

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is way too on-the-nose for what you want to do. Even SCP-2557 had some degree of subtlety to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like format screws

Not this one though. It's not all that interesting and I don't even know what the anomaly is in the first place. The images are kinda nice, but that's not enough to save it for me

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm getting tired of format screws.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm even more impressed if you wrote and edited it on mobile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope! That statue expunged the hell outta that poor guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my first thoughts for it was to put each picture on an opposite side to give sort of a brochure-y feel, but alas, I am having to work purely through mobile and therefore don't feel terribly confident that I could make it look right except on mobile. I'll almost certainly take a look at it again when I have full 'net access back, but in the meantime I would not be averse to anyone else looking into getting it all formatted to be more aesthetically pleasing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thankya kindly!

I figured this was gonna be one of those things that people either love or hate, so I wasn't terribly surprised to see the votes split pretty much right down the middle at first. I decided to take a shower to get the "I just posted something holy crap holy crap" anxiety sweat off, and I was so shocked at the sudden turnaround when I got out that I did a towel-draped little dance and cheer, which did not impress my dog very much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I would like to try to solve the puzzle.

Spoilers?
Am I correct?

So its a building that was built by the foundation to trap anomalies/GOI members in a vacation experience to contain them. However, it doesn't work because they aren't interested in it or the trap is too obvious. Then the A.I. of the building/thoughts? is/are sad because it doesn't fulfill it's prime directive.

I might be wrong, or maybe fallowing a false trail. I upvoted anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should that be [EXPUNGED] rather than ‘expunged’ in the image caption of the person on the chair?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My interp was there was some sort of containment breach or accident that caused the site to become this resort, and the final pleases both refer to the residents Anomalous need to serve and to their desperation to be saved from the situation. I’m curious to what the author thought up though, as it’s definitely a compelling mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this! Personally, I would try to align the images with their respective paragraphs aside from just shoving them to the right, but I don't know how hard/feasible that would be.

So, yeah, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, because it's genuinely a well written and funny idea, but I am a little confused as to what's happening in-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, okay, I really like this. There was a moment I thought "oh god another format screw", but that was quickly replaced by a moment when I thought "oh, okay, this is pretty nice", and a final moment when I thought "oh wow this is great". Upvote for the world's most secure holiday destination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this.

Part of the reason is because it incorporates such high-quality images.
Part of the reason is because it is comprehensive in its considerations of the guest(s).
Part of the reason is because it keeps me guessing as to whether this is the scip or the containment procedures for one.

Which begs for a reread.
And a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah fuck it why not.

Edit: fuck me I actually love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I quite like the image this presents and the implications therein. It seems like reception has been very mixed so far, but I commend your creativity. The ending was also just the right amount of melodrama for my personal tastes. +1

Edit: and there’s the positive reception this deserves!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
You can check out anytime you like
But you can never leave

Translations
Spanish by berstager

Image Links

Acid swimming - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/27298
Life of the Unliving - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/1138493
Blood-letting ritual - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/1363462
Vegan munchies - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/1026783
No way out - https://pxhere.com/en/photo/736093

Authortorium | Imaginarium
